setUp().details: Sets up the tests with constants from the ResourceMetering contract.

test_meter_generateArtifact_succeeds().details: Generates a CSV file. No more than the L1 block gas limit should      be supplied to the `meter` function to avoid long execution time.      This test is skipped because there is no need to run it every time.      It generates a CSV file on disk that can be used to analyze the      gas usage and cost of the `ResourceMetering` contract. The next time      that the gas usage needs to be analyzed, the skip may be removed.

userdoc.notice: A table test that sets the state of the ResourceParams and then requests         various amounts of gas. This test ensures that a wide range of values         can safely be used with the `ResourceMetering` contract.         It also writes a CSV file to disk that includes useful information         about how much gas is used and how expensive it is in USD terms to         purchase the deposit gas.

setUp().details: Sets up the tests with constants from the ResourceMetering contract.

test_meter_generateArtifact_succeeds().details: Generates a CSV file. No more than the L1 block gas limit should      be supplied to the `meter` function to avoid long execution time.      This test is skipped because there is no need to run it every time.      It generates a CSV file on disk that can be used to analyze the      gas usage and cost of the `ResourceMetering` contract. The next time      that the gas usage needs to be analyzed, the skip may be removed.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Tests that the initial resource params are set correctly.
@dev Tests that reinitializing the resource params are set correctly.
@dev Tests that updating the resource params to the same values works correctly.
@dev Tests that updating the initial block number sets the meter params correctly.
@dev Tests that updating the initial block number sets the meter params correctly.
@dev Tests that updating the initial block number sets the meter params correctly.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Tests that updating the gas delta sets the meter params correctly.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Tests that the meter params are set correctly for the maximum gas delta.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)
function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Tests that the metered modifier reverts if the value is greater than allowed.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Tests that resource metering can handle large gaps between deposits.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)
function () view external returns (struct ResourceMetering.ResourceConfig memory)

documentation.text: @dev Sets up the tests with constants from the ResourceMetering contract.

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "ResourceMetering: cannot buy more gas than available gas limit"

rightHandSide.value: ResourceMetering: cannot buy more gas than available gas limit

documentation.text:
@dev Generates a CSV file. No more than the L1 block gas limit should
      be supplied to the `meter` function to avoid long execution time.
      This test is skipped because there is no need to run it every time.
      It generates a CSV file on disk that can be used to analyze the
      gas usage and cost of the `ResourceMetering` contract. The next time
      that the gas usage needs to be analyzed, the skip may be removed.

@title ArtifactResourceMetering_Test
 @notice A table test that sets the state of the ResourceParams and then requests
         various amounts of gas. This test ensures that a wide range of values
         can safely be used with the `ResourceMetering` contract.
         It also writes a CSV file to disk that includes useful information
         about how much gas is used and how expensive it is in USD terms to
         purchase the deposit gas.
